project_title: Counterintelligence Surveillance Framework - Beyond Surveillance Maximalism: a Smart Surveillance Scaling (S3) proposal
product_name: Counterintelligence Surveillance Framework - Beyond Surveillance Maximalism: a Smart Surveillance Scaling (S3) proposal
runtime_target: structured public page plus project-specific data packet and proof runner
operational_end_state: A reviewer can inspect what the project is, what it produces, what is built now, and what must be built next.
full_build_definition: A research packet with question, method, evidence, source utilization, analysis plan, limitations, artifact outputs, review gates, and submission or conference-readiness criteria.
minimum_operational_release: A reviewable research packet for Counterintelligence Surveillance Framework - Beyond Surveillance Maximalism: a Smart Surveillance Scaling (S3) proposal: research question, design, evidence map, analysis plan, limitations, source-use table, and next empirical or publication step.
required_modules_to_build: input fixture | operation trace | output artifact | review gate
source_status: 7 source-application rows; 4 active passage contracts; 0 unlocated source candidates.
source_actions_before_claim: Complete 4 close-read contract(s), update 12 linked proof-capsule field(s), and preserve the current claim ceiling until passage notes exist.
test_suite: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence | source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation | privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy
acceptance_tests: Verify that the question, design, evidence, analysis plan, limitations, and source use support only the claims currently made.
promotion_stage: full-build candidate; execute minimum operational release next
current_status: specified proof plan; needs live or replayable demonstrator
launch_or_review_path: Open the project contract for `isc-proposals`, assemble the research packet, and run source, method, and claim-boundary checks.
first_full_build: Add one implementation scenario: policy problem, decision rule, control point, abuse case, measurable criterion, and revision trigger.
next_implementation_steps: 1. create or confirm runtime data schema; 2. implement one end-to-end fixture; 3. capture proof artifact; 4. run source and claim-ceiling gates; 5. publish reviewer-safe demo
public_claim_now: May claim a full-build candidate exists with proof and source contracts; do not claim deployment, outside validation, or production readiness until live execution and passage verification are recorded.
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
